standard: Hồi đấy nhờ cái chải này mà đầu tao đỡ ngứa đây.
A: Back then, this comb helped relieve the itching in my scalp.
B: Back then, thanks to this brush, my head stopped itching.
C: Back then, thanks to this comb, my head was less itchy.

standard: Long Xuyên cũng có bán này, khi nào đám giỗ về ăn.
A: They sell this in Long Xuyen too; I'll eat it when there's a memorial service.
B: Long Xuyên also sells this, come back and eat it during the death anniversary.
C: Long Xuyên also sells this, when there's a death anniversary ceremony, come back to eat.

standard: thương quá mẹ ạ, không khổ nào bằng.
A: Oh, Mom, I feel so sorry for you. There's no suffering like this.
B: I feel so much pity, Mom; there is no suffering worse than this.
C: I love you so much, Mom. No suffering compares to this.

standard: Thật tình, tôi không biết nhé
A: Honestly, I don't know.
B: Honestly, I don't know.
C: Honestly, I really don't know, you know.

standard: Đi gần bên em đi. Do Gò Vấp nó nhiều chỗ đi hơn.
A: Go somewhere closer to me. Because Go Vap has more places to go.
B: Walk near me. Because Go Vap has more places to go.
C: Go near me. Gò Vấp has more places to go anyway.

standard: Ngon quá, có ai mua không nhỉ?
A: It looks delicious, would anyone like to buy some?
B: So delicious, I wonder if anyone is buying it?
C: So delicious, is anyone interested in buying it?

standard: có sổ hộ nghèo, họ có cho đi thử không nhỉ?
A: If I have a poverty certificate, will they let me try it out?
B: If I have a poor household registration book, will they let me take the test?
C: If I have a poor household certificate, would they let me try taking the test?

standard: nói không xuể, cả 1 rỗ đây.
A: There are too many to list, it's a whole basketful.
B: I can't even list them all, there's a whole basket of them here.
C: I can't even describe it all, there's a whole tray here.

standard: Miền Bắc khổ cực kỳ cụ Đại ơi
A: The North is suffering terribly, Grandpa Dai!
B: The North is suffering terribly, Great-grandfather.
C: The North is incredibly miserable, dear Đại.

standard: Bữa nay đi nhậu không thấy cảnh sát giao thông thổi nồng độ cồn nhỉ?
A: I didn't see any traffic police checking for drunk driving today while I was out drinking, did I?
B: I didn't see any traffic police conducting breathalyzer tests while going out drinking today, did I?
C: Today when I went drinking, I didn't see any traffic police doing breathalyzer tests, huh?

standard: Kì dáng mi nhon mất từ khi nào không biết. Bây giờ đoạn thiếu đoạn thừa nhìn khiếp đảm
A: I don't know when she lost her slender figure. Now, she looks horrifying with her missing and excess parts.
B: I don't know when my slim figure disappeared. Now, with some parts lacking and others excessive, it looks terrible.
C: I don't know when the thin brow shape disappeared. Now with patches missing and patches overgrown, it looks terrifying

standard: cũng sắp đi chợ rồi chứ, mà không dậy nổi.
A: I was about to go to the market, but I couldn't get up.
B: It's almost time to go to the market, but I can't get myself up.
C: I'm about to go to the market, but I can't get up.

standard: Tướng mày mà đậu xách xe ra đường đi ngớp ngớp vậy đền họ không nổi
A: If you, General, park your car and drive around like that, you won't be able to afford to compensate them.
B: With a forehead like that, if you take the bike out on the road and cause an accident, you won't be able to afford the compensation.
C: Look at you, if you park and drive that badly on the road, you won't even be able to compensate them

standard: mày làm gì xấu hổ vậy em
A: What are you doing that's so embarrassing, girl?
B: What are you doing that's so embarrassing?
C: What embarrassing thing did you do?

standard: Bữa nay về quê mà quên không mang bằng lái
A: I went back to my hometown today but forgot to bring my driver's license.
B: I went back to my hometown today but forgot to bring my driver's license.
C: Today I went back to my hometown but forgot to bring my driver's license.

standard: anh em mình hết mình luôn nhé chúng mày
A: Let's give it our all, guys!
B: Let's give it our all, you guys.
C: We'll go all out for sure, you guys!

standard: Mặt mũi này trung niên chứ thanh niên gì?
A: This face looks like he's middle-aged, not young.
B: This face looks middle-aged, not youthful.
C: This face of mine is clearly middle-aged, not young anymore.

standard: về quê ăn Tết này sắp nhỏ ơi.
A: Let's go back to our hometown for Tet this year, kids.
B: Going back to the hometown for Tet, kids.
C: Going back to the countryside to celebrate this Tet soon, little one.

standard: Bạn về với bố mẹ à?
A: Are you going back to your parents' house?
B: Are you going back to your parents?
C: Are you going back to your parents?

standard: Chỗ nào đây bà con nhỉ?
A: Where is this place, folks?
B: Where is this, everyone?
C: Where is this, folks?

standard: mê thật cái sắc đỏ của lon nhé.
A: I really love the red color of the can.
B: I really love the red color of the can.
C: I really love the red color of that can.

standard: Xã nào mà có sáu chị em gái, mấy cô chỉ được cái nói bậy, không ai nghe ai đâu
A: In a village with six sisters, all they do is talk nonsense; nobody listens to anyone else.
B: Which commune has six sisters, where the ladies only know how to talk nonsense and no one listens to anyone else?
C: Which village would have six sisters - those women only know how to talk nonsense, and no one listens to anyone.

standard: Nướng kiểu gì mà để cháy đen thui vậy chúng mày nhỉ.
A: What kind of grilling is this? It's completely burnt black!
B: How did you guys grill it to make it completely burnt like that?
C: How did you guys grill it to get it all burnt black like that?

standard: chỉ có nhà giàu có được thôi, còn chỗ tôi vẫn tự nấu.
A: Only rich people can afford that; where I live, I still cook for myself.
B: Only rich people can afford that; as for my place, I still cook for myself.
C: Only rich people can afford it, as for me I still cook at home.

standard: Đi đâu thì cút gấp đi chúng mày ơi.
A: Get out of here, you guys!
B: Wherever you're going, get the hell out of here quickly, guys.
C: Just get lost quickly wherever you're going, all of you.

standard: Trung thu gì tầm này, ở phòng mà ngủ.
A: What's the point of celebrating Mid-Autumn Festival at this time? Just stay in your room and sleep.
B: What Mid-Autumn Festival at this time, just stay in the room and sleep.
C: What kind of Mid-Autumn Festival is this, just sleeping in the room.

standard: chỗ này buổi sáng sang trường chị không có tàu cũng đã sợ rồi.
A: Even in the mornings, when there's no train to get to my sister's school, I'm already scared.
B: In this place, I'm already scared when there are no trains to go to school in the morning.
C: I'm already scared coming to school in the morning here, even without a train.

standard: Dân Hà Tĩnh ta sang bên đó làm ăn nhiều.
A: Many people from Ha Tinh go over there to do business.
B: Many of our Ha Tinh people go over there to work.
C: Many of our people from Hà Tĩnh go over there to work and do business.

standard: cái gì lạ vậy chị Phương nhỉ?
A: What's that strange thing, Ms. Phuong?
B: What's so strange, Ms. Phuong?
C: What's so strange about it, Sister Phương?

standard: Nhanh còn chậm hết, không mấy khi có đâu
A: Hurry, while supplies last! These are rare opportunities.
B: Hurry up or it'll be gone; this doesn't happen often.
C: Even the fast ones are all slow now, there's hardly ever any that are actually fast.

standard: không có hướng dẫn đâu mà ngày nào cũng bày đặt vô coi
A: There are no instructions, so why do you keep checking every day?
B: There are no instructions, yet you keep pretending to come in and check every day.
C: There are no instructions, yet every day I make up random stuff to watch.

standard: Sao mà dại dột vậy chị? Tiền tiêu không hết thì kêu em tiêu phụ cho.
A: How could you be so foolish, sister? If you have extra money, just ask me to help you spend it.
B: Why were you so foolish, sister? If you have more money than you can spend, just tell me and I'll help you spend it.
C: Why are you so foolish, sis? If you can't finish spending your money, just let me help you spend it.

standard: Không hiểu sao nhưng mà tao cứ thích chơi một mình chúng mày ạ
A: I don't know why, but I just love playing alone, guys.
B: I don't know why, but I just like playing alone, you guys.
C: I don't know why, but I just like hanging out alone, you guys.

standard: Lo xuống đi đã. Trên này lịch khi nào cũng rảnh.
A: Come down first. My schedule is always free up here.
B: Just go down first. My schedule up here is always free.
C: Just go down first. I'm always free up here.

standard: tao đâu có ốm tới mức đó.
A: I'm not that sick.
B: I'm not that sick.
C: I'm not that sick.

standard: Khi đó trần truồng luôn nhỉ?
A: They were all naked back then, weren't they?
B: Were you completely naked then?
C: So you were naked at that time too, right?

standard: Con này nó có biết gì đâu. Người trong ao hồ chưa ra biển lớn được.
A: This thing doesn't know anything. People who live in ponds and lakes haven't yet ventured into the open sea.
B: This one doesn't know anything. People in a pond haven't reached the open sea yet.
C: This kid doesn't know anything at all. Someone who's only been in a pond hasn't made it to the big sea yet.

standard: Nhìn nhiều người mua hay sao mà 2 ngày nay kho không có bánh, làm em không có để giao cho mọi người
A: Seeing how many people wanted to buy them, the warehouse has been out of stock for the past two days, so I don't have any to deliver to everyone.
B: It seems like so many people bought them that the warehouse has been out of cakes for the past two days, so I have none to deliver to everyone.
C: It seems like there are so many people buying, but for the past 2 days the warehouse has no cakes, so I have nothing to deliver to everyone.

standard: Năm này không còn đứa nào nữa.
A: There are none left this year.
B: There are no more kids left this year.
C: There are no more of them this year.

standard: Chưa thấy nhà nào toàn con gái hết mà khổ cả. Con trai hết mới khổ.
A: I've never seen a family with only daughters that's miserable. It's when all the children are sons that it's truly difficult.
B: I've never seen a family with only daughters that is miserable. It's when there are only sons that it's miserable.
C: I've never seen any family with all daughters that has it tough. It's having all sons that's really hard.

standard: Ai biết, bữa qua có cái gì đâu.
A: Who knows, there was nothing like that yesterday.
B: Who knows, there wasn't anything there yesterday.
C: I don't know, there was nothing the other day.

standard: Không mưa đâu, nắng bể đầu.
A: It's not raining, it's scorching hot.
B: It's not raining; it's scorching hot.
C: No rain, just scorching sun.

standard: Nước đó nghèo khủng khiếp có quả cam cũng bâu lại chia.
A: That country was terribly poor; they would even huddle together to share an orange.
B: That country is terribly poor; even if there is an orange, they all crowd around to share it.
C: That country is extremely poor, even when they have an orange, they still gather together to share it.

standard: Hỏi ngớ ngẩn. Mày có biết không mà hỏi người khác.
A: What a silly question. Do you even know the answer yourself? Why are you asking someone else?
B: Silly question. Do you even know the answer, yet you're asking others?
C: What a silly question. Do you even know (the answer) that you have to ask others?

standard: Quảng Ninh giàu tình nghĩa, giàu tiền bạc.
A: Quang Ninh is rich in kindness and rich in money.
B: Quang Ninh is rich in both affection and money.
C: Quảng Ninh is rich in heartfelt bonds and rich in wealth.

standard: Mình tung tăng vượt ruộng sông qua Thuận An thăm cô bé nhé?
A: Shall we happily skip across the fields and rivers to Thuan An to visit the little girl?
B: Shall we frolic across the fields and rivers to Thuan An to visit the little girl?
C: I'm skipping across fields and rivers to Thuận An to visit the girl, okay?

standard: Đang coi thằn lằn bám kính thật đẹp. Sương mù xuống che lại hết sạch!
A: I was admiring the lizard clinging to the windowpane; it was so beautiful. Then the fog came down and covered it all up!
B: I was watching a lizard clinging to the glass, it looked so beautiful. Then the fog rolled in and covered everything!
C: I'm watching a monitor lizard clinging to the glass, it's so beautiful. The fog came down and covered it all completely!

standard: Lúc đó cháu tưởng vậy là ngầu.
A: At the time, I thought that was cool.
B: At that time, I thought that was cool.
C: Back then, I thought that was cool.

standard: Chị chịu thì chở em đi đâu cũng được.
A: If you agree, you can take me anywhere you want.
B: If you're willing, you can drive me anywhere.
C: If you're willing, take me anywhere you want.

standard: khi nào có 10 triệu mới lấy, không tham lam đâu
A: I'll only take it when I have 10 million, I'm not greedy.
B: I'll only take it when there are 10 million; I'm not being greedy.
C: I'll only get it when I have 10 million, I'm not being greedy

standard: Đúng đấy, năm 2010, tôi đi xe máy mà nước xô ngã lăn quay ướt sũng từ đầu tới chân.
A: That's right, in 2010, I was riding my motorbike and the water rushed over me, causing me to fall and get soaked from head to toe.
B: That's right, in 2010, I was riding a motorbike when the water knocked me over, and I was soaked from head to toe.
C: That's right, in 2010, I was riding a motorcycle when water knocked me down and I rolled around, getting soaking wet from head to toe.

standard: Gì mà tội vậy trời ơi!
A: Oh my God, what a terrible crime!
B: Oh my god, that's so pitiful!
C: That's so unfair!

standard: còn đầy ông nhảy xuống đây tắm.
A: There are still plenty of guys jumping in here to swim.
B: There are still plenty of guys jumping down here to swim.
C: There's still plenty of water, jump down here to swim.

standard: Bà có về không mà bà hay bàn quá vây.
A: Is she even coming home? She talks so much about it.
B: Are you even coming back, or why are you talking so much?
C: Are you going home or not, that you're always commenting too much?

standard: ngứa ngứa cái tay rồi đấy nhé chú.
A: My hands are getting itchy, uncle.
B: My hands are starting to itch, you know.
C: My hand is getting itchy now, Uncle.

standard: Đẹp gì đâu. Huế mà mưa là đói chết luôn, chứ có gì mà đẹp.
A: There's nothing beautiful about it. When it rains in Hue, you'll starve to death, so there's nothing beautiful about it.
B: What's so beautiful about it. When it rains in Hue, you'll starve to death; there's nothing beautiful about it.
C: It's not beautiful at all. When it rains in Hue, you literally starve to death, so what's beautiful about that.

standard: muốn đi liền ngay bây giờ luôn này.
A: I want to go right now!
B: I want to go right now.
C: I want to go right now.

standard: Thế 5 đứa có giúp mẹ gì không?
A: Did the five kids help their mother with anything?
B: So did the five of you help your mother with anything?
C: So do the 5 of you help your mom with anything?

standard: Có thật thế này thì dân Hà Nội không ngại Sài Gòn nữa đâu nhỉ?
A: If this were true, people in Hanoi wouldn't be afraid of Saigon anymore, would they?
B: If it's really like this, then Hanoians won't be intimidated by Saigon anymore, right?
C: If it's really like this, Hanoi people won't have any trouble with Saigon anymore, right?

standard: Trời lạnh ăn 1 dĩa tuyệt vời thật chứ.
A: Eating a plate of this on a cold day is absolutely wonderful.
B: Eating a plate of this in the cold weather is truly wonderful.
C: It's cold, and eating a plate is truly wonderful.

standard: Xe đó khoảng hai triệu, tầm tuần con về mẹ nhé, mẹ đừng khóc mà mệt
A: That car costs about two million dong, I'll be home in about a week, Mom. Don't cry, it'll only tire you out.
B: That bike is about two million; I'll be home in about a week, Mom, so please don't cry and make yourself tired.
C: That car is about two million, I'll come back in about a week, Mom, okay? Don't cry, it makes you tired.

standard: Xe mà chở 4, 5. Sao dễ sợ vậy nhỉ?
A: A car carrying 4 or 5 people. How scary is that?
B: The car is carrying 4 or 5 people. How scary is that?
C: A vehicle carrying 4-5 people. Why is it so scary though?

standard: Cá đó ăn ngon tuyệt vời luôn.
A: That fish tastes absolutely delicious.
B: That fish tastes absolutely wonderful.
C: That fish was incredibly delicious.

standard: Trả chứ tao không lấy, con rể tặng thì lấy chứ của chúng mày lấy làm gì
A: I'll give it back, I'm not taking it. If my son-in-law gave it to me, I'll take it, but what would I do with what you guys would take?
B: I'll return it because I'm not taking it; if my son-in-law gave it, I'd take it, but why would I take it from you guys?
C: Give it back then, I won't take it. If the son-in-law gives it, I'll take it. Why would I take yours?

standard: Mẹ tớ vẫn đang đi uống nước chè, chưa sao cả.
A: My mom is still out drinking tea, everything's fine.
B: My mom is still out drinking tea, she's fine.
C: My mom is still out drinking tea, she's fine.

standard: Du lịch ở đâu bằng du lịch chỗ này.
A: Nowhere is better than this place for tourism.
B: No other travel destination compares to this place.
C: Nowhere else compares to traveling here.

standard: Sao giờ kể nghe chuyện đau lòng vậy trời?
A: Why are you telling such a heartbreaking story now?
B: Why are you telling such a heartbreaking story now?
C: Why are there so many heartbreaking stories going around now?

standard: Cầu mong có phép màu đến với các cháu.
A: I pray for a miracle to happen for these children.
B: I pray that a miracle comes to the children.

standard: bò to vậy mà mất nhanh nhỉ?
A: That cow was so big, how could it have disappeared so quickly?
B: The cow was so big, yet it disappeared so quickly, didn't it?
C: It's so big but it disappeared quickly, huh?

standard: Ở gần mới chạy lui chạy tới coi con ra sao. Chứ xa làm sao mà chạy
A: If you live nearby, you can run back and forth to check on your child. But if you live far away, how can you run?
B: I'm only staying nearby so I can go back and forth to see how the baby is doing. If I were far away, there's no way I could rush back.
C: Only when it's nearby can I run back and forth to check on it. But if it's far, how can I even run there?

standard: Vậy là biết anh em quê chúng tôi chưa.
A: So now you know who we're from, guys?
B: So now you know where we are from.
C: So, do you already know my hometown friends?

standard: Cha làm món này là ngon tuyệt vời!
A: My dad makes this dish absolutely delicious!
B: Dad makes this dish and it's absolutely wonderful!
C: My dad makes this dish and it's absolutely delicious!

standard: giờ rủ nó không đi đâu, phải rủ đi uống nước ngọt mới chịu đi
A: Now, if you invite her anywhere, she won't go; you have to invite her for a soft drink for her to agree to go.
B: If I invite them to go anywhere now, they won't go; I have to invite them to go drink soft drinks for them to agree to go.
C: Now inviting them doesn't work at all, I have to ask them to go get some soda first before they'll agree to come.

standard: người bữa nay đông thật bà nhỉ?
A: There are so many people today, aren't there, Grandma?
B: There are really a lot of people today, aren't there?
C: It's really crowded today, isn't it?

standard: Con này mà nửa đêm đi đường không bật đèn chắc té ngã quá
A: If this car were to drive on the road at midnight without its headlights on, it would probably fall.
B: If this thing is driven on the road at midnight without turning on the lights, it will probably crash and fall.
C: This person would probably trip and fall if they walked around at midnight without turning on any lights.

standard: Chừng đấy mày già rồi
A: You're getting old by now.
B: By then, you'll be old.
C: That's already old for you

standard: Kệ, ế ế mà đều đều một ngày chục hộp được. Chứ đắc quá sợ giàu tiêu không hết, vậy nhé
A: Never mind, even if it's slow, I can still make about ten boxes a day. If it were too expensive, I'd be too rich to spend it all, so that's that.
B: Whatever, being slow is fine as long as I can sell a dozen boxes a day. If it's too expensive, I'm afraid I'll be so rich that I won't be able to spend it all, so let's leave it at that.
C: Never mind, even though it's slow, I still steadily sell about ten boxes a day. It's so good—I'm afraid I won't be able to spend all that money. Alright then.

standard: Còn chứ, sao quên được. Bất chấp nước lụt, cầm dép lội đi luôn mà. Quên gì nổi.
A: Of course, how could I forget? Despite the flood, I still waded through it with my sandals. How could I possibly forget?
B: Of course, how could I forget. Despite the floodwaters, I just held my sandals and waded right through. There's no way I could forget.
C: Of course, how could I forget. Despite the floods, I just grabbed my sandals and walked through anyway. How could anyone forget.

standard: ăn ăn suốt ngày, sau này lăn như mấy con heo.
A: Eating all day long, later on you'll be rolling around like a pig.
B: Eating all day long, later you'll be rolling around like some pigs.
C: Eats all day long, later will end up rolling around like pigs.

standard: Cuối tuần là về rồi chứ gì nữa.
A: We're going home this weekend, aren't we?
B: I'm going home this weekend, obviously.
C: Weekend's here already.

standard: Ngứa người, thèm đi rồi mà giờ đang bận chưa đi được.
A: I'm itching to go out, but I'm too busy right now.
B: I'm itching to go, but I'm busy right now and can't.
C: I'm feeling restless and really want to go already, but I'm busy right now so I can't.

standard: Đi Hà Bội là đi đâu nhỉ?
A: Where exactly is Ha Boi?
B: Where exactly is Ha Boi?
C: Where exactly is Hà Bội?

standard: Nướng kiểu Pháp nên màu đẹp nhỉ?
A: The French-style baking gives it a beautiful color, doesn't it?
B: It's French-style roasting, so the color looks beautiful, doesn't it?
C: French-style roasting should make such a nice color, right?

standard: Bận quá nghỉ phép 2, 3 bữa thôi sẵn về ăn cơm ngoại nấu luôn.
A: I'm too busy, so I'll just take 2 or 3 days off and enjoy my mom's home-cooked meals.
B: I'm so busy, so I'll just take 2 or 3 days off and take the opportunity to go home and eat my grandma's cooking.
C: I've been so busy, so I'll just take 2 or 3 days off to go home and eat the food my grandmother is cooking.

standard: Bây giờ không cẩn thận, vợ nó về nhà bà ngoại là toi đấy bạn.
A: If you're not careful now, and his wife goes back to her grandmother's house, you're doomed.
B: If you're not careful now, you're doomed once his wife goes back to her grandmother's house, my friend.

standard: anh chắc có chuyện với chị của em quá, mà công nhận cà phê dừa này ngon thật.
A: He must have had some kind of problem with my sister, but I have to admit, this coconut coffee is really delicious.
B: I think something is going on between you and my sister, but I have to admit this coconut coffee is really delicious.
C: He probably has something going on with my sister's friend, but I have to admit this coconut coffee is really delicious.

standard: Chân cô sao rồi, có bị gì lắm không?
A: How is your leg? Is it seriously injured?
B: How is your leg? Is it seriously injured?
C: How's your foot, is it badly injured?

standard: đoạn này ngã tư giao nhau rộng lắm, mà chưa có đèn xanh đỏ cô à
A: This intersection is very wide, and there are no traffic lights yet, ma'am.
B: This intersection is very wide, but there are no traffic lights yet, ma'am.
C: This four-way intersection here is really wide, but there aren't any traffic lights yet, auntie.

standard: em chỉ công nhận hơi đểu với cái miệng không ai nói lại thôi nhé.
A: I only admit that I'm a bit mean with my mouth, and nobody can argue with that.
B: I only admit that I'm a bit sly and that no one can outtalk me.
C: I only admit I'm a bit cheeky with my mouth, just because no one can talk back, that's all.

standard: Ở Đồng Hới đầy đường em ơi. Nhặt không hết.
A: There are tons of them on the streets of Dong Hoi, my dear. You can't pick them all up.
B: There are plenty of them all over the streets in Dong Hoi, honey. There are too many to pick up.
C: In Đồng Hới there are so many [seafood] all over the roads, dear. You can't pick them all up.

standard: Quá tuyệt vời. Có ai làm được vậy nữa không nhỉ?
A: That's amazing. Is there anyone else who can do that?
B: Absolutely wonderful. I wonder if anyone else can do that?
C: That's absolutely amazing. Is there anyone else who can do that?

standard: Sớm giờ được con cá nào chưa?
A: Have you caught any fish yet this morning?
B: Have you caught any fish yet this morning?
C: Have you caught any fish yet this early?

standard: Ở trong Kỳ Anh thì phải chị à, mà thấy nói là tối nay với mai mưa to nữa.
A: It's in Ky Anh, I think, and I heard it's going to rain heavily tonight and tomorrow.
B: It's in Ky Anh, I think, but I heard that it's going to rain heavily tonight and tomorrow too.
C: Being in Ky Anh, you know, but I heard there'll be heavy rain again tonight and tomorrow.

standard: người ta nói đúng là nhìn trường người ta thật ham trường mình chán thật chứ!
A: They say it's true, you can't help but envy other people's schools, and you can't even imagine how disappointing your own is!
B: People are right when they say that looking at other people's schools makes you envious; my school is truly boring!
C: They say it's true - seeing other schools really makes me envious while my own school is just so boring!

standard: Chị thì đi nhiều nơi xa hơn nữa nhỉ?
A: You've traveled to even more distant places, haven't you?
B: You've traveled to even more distant places, haven't you?
C: Wow, so you get to travel to even more faraway places, huh?

standard: Ế nấm rồi. Ai cứu em với chứ ăn không hết rồi
A: I'm out of mushrooms! Someone please save me, I can't eat them all!
B: I have leftover mushrooms. Someone please save me, I can't eat them all.
C: I have so many mushrooms left. Someone help me, I can't finish eating them all.

standard: Cái bánh này mà ném vào mặt nyc chắc ở Hà Nội cũng nghe thấy nhỉ?
A: If this cake were thrown in my ex's face, I bet people in Hanoi would hear about it, right?
B: If I threw this cake at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it, wouldn't they?
C: This cake, if I threw it at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it too, huh?

standard: chờ tao gặt ít lúa bên này đã, rồi về lại dắt đi
A: Wait, let me harvest some rice over here first, then I'll come back and take you there.
B: Wait for me to harvest some rice over here first, then I'll come back and take you.
C: Wait for me to harvest some rice on this side first, then I'll come back and lead you along

standard: Thế à, thế là nó không đi học được rồi.
A: Oh, so he can't go to school then.
B: Is that so? Then it means they can't go to school.
C: Oh I see, so they can't go to school anymore.

standard: Ngon không? Để hôm nào tao đi thử.
A: Is it delicious? I'll go try it sometime.
B: Is it good? I'll go try it sometime.
C: Is it delicious? I'll go try it sometime.

standard: Thôi ai đi thì đi chứ tôi thì chịu.
A: Whoever wants to go, go ahead, but I can't.
B: Whatever, whoever wants to go can go, but I can't do it.
C: Fine, whoever wants to go can go, but I'm not going.

standard: Tôi không phải sinh viên mà tôi cũng biết đây này bạn.
A: Even though I'm not a student, I know this.
B: I'm not a student, but even I know this.
C: I'm not even a student, but I know this too, you know.

standard: Ngày trước chị học dưới này chắc cũng ngập như thế này nhỉ?
A: Back when you studied down here, it must have been flooded just like this, right?
B: Back then, when you studied down here, it was probably flooded like this too, right?
C: I bet it was also flooded like this when you studied here before, right?

standard: Kho tiêu ăn ngon tuyệt vời.
A: The braised pork belly is absolutely delicious.
B: The braised pepper pork tastes wonderful.
C: Pepper braised dish is incredibly delicious.

standard: Ai mà chơi gì lạ vậy nhỉ?
A: Who plays such a strange game?
B: Who is playing something so strange?
C: Who does something so weird like that?

standard: Đoạn này thấy bất ổn nhỉ?
A: This part seems a bit off, doesn't it?
B: This part seems a bit off, doesn't it?
C: This part seems off, huh?

standard: Ông ấy chụp mà nhìn mặt ông ấy nản chưa!
A: Look how disappointed he looks in that photo!
B: He took the photo, but look at how discouraged his face looks!
C: He took (the photo) and looking at his face, isn't he discouraged yet!

standard: Nhìn thúng bánh mướt mà thèm kinh!
A: Looking at that basket of rice noodles makes me crave them so much!
B: Looking at that basket of steamed rice rolls makes me crave them so much!
C: Looking at the basket of bánh mướt makes me crave it so badly!

standard: Bao giờ mới có cơ hội để đi nhỉ?
A: When will I have the chance to go?
B: When will I ever have the chance to go?
C: When will I get a chance to go?

standard: Nghệ An uống bia như nước mà bạn sao đầu hàng sớm thế?
A: People in Nghe An drink beer like water, so why did you give up so quickly?
B: People in Nghe An drink beer like water, so why are you giving up so early?
C: Nghệ An drinks beer like water, but why did you give up so early?

standard: Khi nào có đi chị chỉ quán ngon cho.
A: When you go out, I'll recommend a good restaurant.
B: When you go, I'll show you some good restaurants.
C: When we go, show me a good restaurant.

standard: Thấy quen đấy mà không biết nhà ai cả.
A: It looks familiar, but I can't tell whose house it is.
B: It looks familiar, but I don't know whose house it is.
C: That looks familiar, but I don't know whose house it is.

standard: Mon Lai 2 làm gì có đường Nguyễn Du
A: Mon Lai 2 doesn't have Nguyen Du street.
B: There is no Nguyen Du Street in Mon Lai 2.
C: Mon Lai 2 definitely doesn't have a Nguyen Du street

standard: Không thấy thì là họ thất nghiệp à, rồi lấy gì mà ăn hả em.
A: If you don't see them, does that mean they're unemployed? And what will they eat then?
B: If they aren't seen, does that mean they're unemployed, and then what will they have to eat?
C: If you can't see them, it means they're unemployed, so what will they have to live on, dear?

standard: Cá heo, cá này mà đem kho tiêu cho cay rồi luộc đậu bắp chấm ăn là tuyệt vời
A: Dolphin fish, braised with spicy peppers and served with boiled okra as a dipping sauce, is absolutely delicious.
B: Dolphins; if this fish is braised with pepper to make it spicy and eaten with boiled okra for dipping, it would be wonderful.
C: Dolphin, if we braise this fish with pepper to make it spicy and then boil okra for dipping, it would be amazing

standard: Nhớ là mới làm thì phải, hôm bữa đi một lần mà không thấy.
A: I remember they just started this place; I went there the other day but didn't see it.
B: I remember it was just done, I went there once the other day but didn't see it.
C: Remember, it must be newly made, the other day I went once and didn't see it.

standard: Nhanh làm gì mà khổ vậy không biết
A: Why rush when you can do it so badly?
B: I don't know why I'm rushing so much just to make things hard for myself.
C: Why do you rush like that and suffer for it, I don't understand

standard: không thấy em Linh đâu anh nhỉ?
A: I don't see Linh anywhere, do you?
B: I don't see Linh anywhere, do I?
C: I don't see Linh anywhere, do you?

standard: bài ca muôn thuở, nói thôi chứ có thực hiện đâu
A: It's the same old story, all talk, no action.
B: The same old story; they just talk but never actually do it.
C: Same old empty promises - just talking but never actually following through

standard: Mấy người ngồi xem nhịn cười được hay thật.
A: Those people watching were really good at keeping their laughter to themselves.
B: The people watching were really good at holding back their laughter.
C: You guys can really hold back your laughter sitting there watching, huh.

A: You'll support me, won't you, uncle?

C: You support me, right?

B: I just saw him at the market the other day, and now he's already out there?
C: I just saw him at the market the other day, and now he's already gone outside there? Is that right?

standard: Con mực này trông to nhỉ.
A: This squid looks big.

C: This squid looks big, huh.

A: The girls from Hung Nguyen are so pretty!
B: Girls from Hung Nguyen are pretty, aren't they!
C: The girl from Hưng Nguyên is pretty, right!

A: Wow, how is that white car moving? Amazing!
B: Oh, how is that white car able to move like that? That's really skillful.

B: Seeing all the hype with the check-ins, it means they've actually returned, right?

standard: Nhìn thì đẹp nhưng mà bẩn nhé.

B: It looks beautiful, but it's dirty.
C: It looks nice but it's dirty, though.

B: I really want to record my husband's voice for you guys to hear.

A: I'm so sad!
B: I'm so sad!
C: I'm so sad, huh!

A: Being far away is tough; you have no choice but to go without food if you want to.
B: Being far away is also miserable; if I want to eat it, I just have to accept it.

A: Why does Nghe An smell so good?

A: I can drive any car, why would I need a test drive? I just need the card.

A: You're exaggerating too much right now.
B: You're bluffing way too much right now.
C: You're exaggerating way too much right now, you know.

B: The press has stepped in, so this must be settled once and for all.
C: When the press gets involved in a matter, it must establish justice.

A: So, that couple has already divorced.

C: So this couple also got divorced.

standard: Nhức hết cả đầu!
A: My head hurts so much!
B: My head is killing me!
C: My head is pounding!

standard: Uống một lon là hợp lý luôn!
A: Drinking one can is perfectly reasonable!
B: Drinking one can is just right!
C: Drinking one can is really reasonable!

C: that gambling scumbag, the progenitor of that poor wretch, always likes to take their house and make it a pigsty for his own home

A: I was just teasing you the other day to scare you, relax, at worst we'll go back to farming and tending buffaloes.

standard: Của em thì lấy về đi, không ai nói gì đâu.
A: If it's yours, just take it back; nobody will say anything.
B: As for mine, just take it back; no one will say anything.

A: I heard that Typhoon Tra Mi has turned around.

A: My friend went over there and is now gone for good.

A: E, you guys aren't trying to wake me up from the dead.
B: Don't wake me up from the dead, you guys.
C: Don't wake me up, you guys.

B: Where is the lady selling it, admin? Could you please provide the specific location?

B: Anything is fine, except for that one habit.
C: I can accept anything, it's just that one habit that gets me.

standard: không cần đi đâu cho xa, đây rồi này.

A: My Hue, it's so sad, but it's still beautiful. I love it so much.

B: It's raining and pitch black, so why did you edit it to be so bright?

standard: Kịch khung hết cho tôi, không có đứa nào có tình tiết giảm nhẹ hết.

standard: Chưa đi bao giờ.

C: I've never been there.

standard: Cho đi mổ lợn nhé, mặt thằng này nhìn sát nhân quá.

standard: tự nhiên lại bị thèm nhỉ.

C: Where, who are you in that picture? How come I don't recognize you?
